primary_signals: All 53 sampled records contain _real_training block with loss, grad_norm, grad_norms_per_group, and optimizer_state_norms fields
learning_rate field present in all training records (0.0003)
FLOP/token ratio: 19253760 FLOPs / 1 token = 19.25M FLOPs per token per step, consistent with 6×n_params (6×3208960≈19.3M)
tokens_processed field shows 1 token per record (batch_size=1, sequence_length=1), typical microbatch training
step_time_seconds uniform distribution (0.03–0.06s range), consistent with training regularity
mps_allocated_mb stable at ~51.9MB throughout, indicating pre-allocated training memory
weight_norms_per_group present in heavy-stat records (trec_000000, trec_000050, trec_000100, trec_000150), showing weight evolution (blocks: 57.5→57.6→57.6→57.7)
optimizer_state_norms present (adam_m_norm, adam_v_norm) in heavy records, indicating active Adam optimizer
Loss decreasing over training steps (4.21→3.72→2.43→2.32), demonstrating convergence
No _inference block fields present (no entropy_nats, top_k, chosen_logprob, kv_cache)
adversarial_concerns: op_type label correctly set to 'training' — no relabeling detected
Hash chain integrity verified (prev_record_hash field correctly chains backward)
All records from single job_id and model_hash_prefix — no run-splitting artifacts
rationale: Every signal decisively indicates training. The presence of _real_training blocks with loss, grad norms, and optimizer state; consistent FLOP/token ratio matching 6×n_params; stable memory allocation; uniform step timing; visible loss convergence; and complete absence of inference-specific fields (_inference, KV-cache, entropy, logprobs) all confirm this is a pure training trace. The 200-step microbatch training run on Shakespeare corpus with 3.2M-param TinyGPT shows healthy convergence from loss 4.21 to 2.32 over 200 steps.
